title: Endocrinology — Diabetes, Thyroid, Adrenal USMLE
description: Diabetes management, thyroid disorders, adrenal disease, pituitary, calcium metabolism. Essential USMLE Step 1 & 2 content.
subject: medicine

cards.back: Fasting glucose ≥ 126 mg/dL, OR 2-hr glucose ≥ 200 on OGTT, OR HbA1c ≥ 6.5%, OR random glucose ≥ 200 + symptoms — confirmed on 2 separate occasions (or one if symptoms)

Decreases hepatic glucose production (↓ gluconeogenesis), improves insulin sensitivity. Contraindicated: eGFR < 30, contrast dye procedures (hold 48h), liver failure, alcoholism. Risk: lactic acidosis

cards.front: SGLT2 inhibitors: mechanism and cardiovascular benefit
cards.back: Block Na+/glucose cotransporter in PCT → glycosuria. Benefits: HbA1c ↓, weight loss, BP ↓, reduced HF hospitalization, reduced renal progression (empagliflozin, canagliflozin, dapagliflozin)

cards.front: GLP-1 receptor agonists: mechanism and benefit
cards.back: Incretin mimetics — stimulate insulin, inhibit glucagon, slow gastric emptying, suppress appetite. Weight loss + CV benefit (liraglutide, semaglutide). Side effect: nausea, pancreatitis risk
Fastest: Lispro/Aspart/Glulisine (onset 15 min). Longest: Glargine/Degludec (peakless, 24+ hours). NPH: intermediate
DKA: glucose 250–500, pH < 7.3, bicarbonate < 18, ketones positive (type 1 DM). HHS: glucose > 600, pH > 7.3, minimal ketones, severe dehydration (type 2 DM)
1. IV saline (normal saline 1L/hr). 2. Insulin drip (after K+ > 3.3). 3. Potassium replacement. 4. Bicarbonate only if pH < 6.9
Symptoms of hypoglycemia + documented low glucose + relief with glucose administration
Primary: ↑ TSH, ↓ free T4. Secondary (pituitary): ↓ TSH, ↓ free T4. Tertiary (hypothalamus): ↓ TRH → ↓ TSH → ↓ T4
Anti-TPO (anti-thyroid peroxidase) and anti-thyroglobulin antibodies. Histology: lymphocytic infiltration + Hürthle cell change
IV levothyroxine (T4) + IV hydrocortisone (adrenal insufficiency may coexist) + supportive care (ventilation, warming)
TSH receptor stimulating antibodies (TRAb/TSI). Diffuse goiter, exophthalmos (proptosis), pretibial myxedema, thyroid bruit
Precipitants: surgery, infection, trauma. Treatment: PTU (blocks synthesis + peripheral conversion) + iodine (Lugol's, wait 1h after PTU) + β-blocker (propranolol) + steroids + supportive
Most common thyroid cancer (80%). Psammoma bodies, Orphan Annie eye nuclei. Spreads via lymphatics. Best prognosis

cards.front: Medullary thyroid cancer: marker and associations
cards.back: From parafollicular C cells → ↑ calcitonin. Associated with MEN-2A and MEN-2B (RET proto-oncogene)
Parathyroid hyperplasia (hypercalcemia), Pituitary adenoma (most commonly prolactinoma), Pancreatic tumors (gastrinoma, insulinoma, VIPoma)
Medullary thyroid cancer + Pheochromocytoma + Parathyroid hyperplasia (RET mutation)
Medullary thyroid cancer + Pheochromocytoma + Mucosal neuromas + Marfanoid habitus (NO parathyroid)

10% bilateral, 10% extra-adrenal, 10% malignant, 10% in children, 10% familial. Classic: episodic headache, hypertension, diaphoresis (5 Hs: Hypertension, Headache, Hyperhidrosis, Hyperglycemia, Heart palpitations)

cards.front: Pheochromocytoma: diagnostic test and presurgical management
cards.back: 24-hour urinary catecholamines/metanephrines or plasma free metanephrines. Pre-op: alpha-blocker FIRST (phenoxybenzamine), then beta-blocker (never beta first — hypertensive crisis)
Syndrome: any cause of excess cortisol. Disease: specifically pituitary ACTH-secreting adenoma
1. 24-hr urinary free cortisol OR 1 mg overnight dexamethasone suppression test (screening). 2. Low-dose DST (confirms). 3. ACTH level. 4. High-dose DST or CRH stim (localization)
Hypertension + hypokalemia + metabolic alkalosis + ↑ aldosterone + ↓ renin. Cause: adrenal adenoma (60%) or bilateral hyperplasia (40%). Diagnosis: aldosterone-to-renin ratio
Primary adrenal insufficiency: ↑ ACTH, ↓ cortisol, ↓ aldosterone. Hyperpigmentation (MSH cross-reactivity), hyponatremia, hyperkalemia, hypotension. Treatment: hydrocortisone + fludrocortisone
IV hydrocortisone 100 mg stat (acts as both glucocorticoid and mineralocorticoid at high doses) + IV saline + glucose

cards.front: Hypercalcemia: most common outpatient vs inpatient cause
cards.back: Outpatient: primary hyperparathyroidism. Inpatient: malignancy (osteolytic or PTHrP-mediated)
↑ Ca2+, ↓ phosphate, ↑ PTH (inappropriately normal or elevated). Most common cause: solitary parathyroid adenoma
PTH resistance (not deficiency). ↓ Ca, ↑ phosphate, ↑ PTH, short stature, short 4th/5th metacarpals, mental disability (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy)
Prolonged QTc interval. Tetany (Chvostek sign = facial muscle twitch with tapping CN VII; Trousseau sign = carpopedal spasm with BP cuff inflation)
Most common pituitary tumor. Galactorrhea + amenorrhea (women), ED + decreased libido (men). Treatment: dopamine agonist (cabergoline or bromocriptine)

cards.front: Growth hormone excess (acromegaly): features and test
cards.back: Adults: coarse facial features, large hands/feet, macroglossia, carpal tunnel, DM, bitemporal hemianopia. Diagnosis: IGF-1 level + glucose suppression test (GH not suppressed)

cards.front: Central diabetes insipidus: cause and treatment
cards.back: ADH (vasopressin) deficiency from posterior pituitary damage (surgery, trauma, tumor). Treatment: desmopressin (DDAVP)

cards.front: SIADH vs diabetes insipidus: Na+ and urine osmolality
cards.back: SIADH: hyponatremia, concentrated urine (> 300 mOsm/kg). DI: hypernatremia, dilute urine (< 300 mOsm/kg)
Hypoparathyroidism + Addison's disease + mucocutaneous candidiasis. Autosomal recessive, AIRE gene mutation
Addison's disease + Hashimoto's/Graves + type 1 DM (most common APS)
24-hour urine 5-HIAA (5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid, serotonin metabolite)
Watery Diarrhea, Hypokalemia, Achlorhydria (WDHA syndrome). Elevated VIP levels
Necrolytic migratory erythema (skin rash), diabetes, anemia, weight loss. Elevated glucagon
72-hour fast → symptomatic hypoglycemia with inappropriately elevated insulin and C-peptide (rules out exogenous insulin). Treat with surgical resection
Osteitis fibrosa cystica — subperiosteal bone resorption of radial side of 2nd/3rd middle phalanges, brown tumors, 'salt and pepper' skull

cards.front: Type 2 DM: cardiovascular risk reduction drugs of choice
cards.back: For HF or CKD: SGLT2 inhibitor (empagliflozin/dapagliflozin). For established CVD: GLP-1 agonist (liraglutide/semaglutide)

cards.front: Maturity-onset diabetes of the young (MODY): features
cards.back: Non-autoimmune diabetes in young, lean patients with family history (autosomal dominant). No insulin dependence. HNF/GCK gene mutations
Spironolactone, cimetidine, finasteride, ketoconazole, marijuana, heroin; Klinefelter syndrome; cirrhosis; hyperthyroidism; testicular tumors (↑ estrogen); idiopathic (puberty)
Children: rickets (soft bones, bowing of legs, rachitic rosary). Adults: osteomalacia (bone pain, muscle weakness, ↑ alkaline phosphatase, ↓ Ca/PO4)
DEXA scan screening: women ≥ 65, men ≥ 70 (or earlier if risk factors). T-score ≤ −2.5 = osteoporosis. Treatment: bisphosphonates (alendronate) first-line

cards.front: Paget's disease of bone: findings and treatment
cards.back: ↑ bone turnover → enlarged, deformed bones. ↑ alkaline phosphatase (normal Ca, PO4, PTH). Skull ('cotton wool' appearance), hearing loss, hat size increase. Treatment: bisphosphonates

cards.front: What hormones use cAMP as second messenger?
cards.back: FSH, LH, ACTH, TSH, CRH, ADH (V2), PTH, glucagon, hCG, calcitonin (FLAT ChAMP)

cards.front: What hormones use IP3/DAG (Ca2+) as second messenger?
cards.back: GnRH, TRH, Oxytocin, ADH (V1), Angiotensin II, Gastrin, Histamine H1, Acetylcholine (Gq-coupled): HAVe (1 cup) of GOAT Milk
